prevalence.trend: stable; recognition improved with antibody testing

vulnerablePopulations: patients with relapsing disease requiring high-efficacy prevention
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: International NMOSD criteria with antibody testing and MRI features; differentiation from MS is standard

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with access to specialized care and biologics; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to AQP4 testing, MRI, and plasmapheresis in specialist centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Relapse steroids/plasmapheresis; maintenance immunotherapy including biologics in specialized care pathways
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access variability across member states and high cost of newer agents
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialist neuroimmunology services and structured relapse-prevention pathways in many systems
